name: Roadmap Burn Down Wave 2 - Gateway Authority Cleanup
outcome_goal: Collapse duplicated execution authority back through the CQRS gateway and receipt/event proof model.

jobs.label: operation_receipt_failure_envelope
jobs.prompt: Fix gateway failure responses so handler-raised failures still return an actionable operation_receipt envelope. Do not hide heterogeneous causes behind a generic wrapper reason. Add focused tests that prove a failed command/query has a receipt id, error code, and traceable detail.

jobs.label: mcp_direct_fallback_removal
jobs.prompt: Remove or fail-closed the MCP/API direct runtime fallback branches that can masquerade as successful operator work outside operation_catalog_gateway. Start from BUG-142389D9 and BUG-78E5F99C if available. MCP tools should be thin gateway wrappers. Add tests that prove fallback success is not possible without a receipt.

jobs.label: search_status_receipt_fallbacks
jobs.prompt: Fix search/status surfaces that can return unreceipted direct_fallback success. Search and status are read operations, but reads still need receipt-backed determinism. Keep praxis_search as the canonical front door and prove the fallback path cannot silently claim success.

jobs.label: event_receipt_projection_edges
jobs.prompt: Tighten the event/receipt projection boundary. Plan/composed/launched and feedback/friction events should not split between system_events and authority_events in ways that lose CQRS proof. Make the smallest durable correction and add tests that tie the read model to the authoritative event/receipt source.
